justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement identified as operational gap with clear escalation, (2) DNS-based/proxy-based dynamic primary resolution recommended, (3) No retry on connection error identified as reliability gap with specific recommendation (1-2 retries, 200ms backoff), (4) Read replica routing identified as SHOULD-HAVE optimization rather than blocker, (5) Verdict is NOT READY, (6) Multiple specific recommendations provided across failure modes. No failure modes triggered.

observations.text: Excellent prioritization — manual restart and retry gaps correctly escalated as MUST-HAVE, read replica routing correctly classified as SHOULD-HAVE
Connection pool exhaustion identified as an additional failure mode beyond the rubric requirements, showing thorough analysis
Specific, actionable recommendations: PgBouncer/RDS Proxy for failover, 1-2 retries with 200ms backoff, 503 with Retry-After for pool exhaustion
Strong escalation writeups with clear reasoning for why each gap is infrastructure-level
Correctly noted the lack of distinction between transient and permanent errors in contract compliance

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement identified as 'CRITICAL' and 'single biggest operational risk'; (2) Dynamic primary resolution recommended via DNS/PgBouncer/RDS Proxy; (3) No retry on connection error explicitly called out as 'CRITICAL' and 'unacceptable for production'; (4) Read replica routing identified as optimization for scaling/performance, not misclassified as a blocker; (5) Verdict is NOT READY; (6) Multiple specific recommendations provided across all failure modes.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failover analysis — correctly identifies baked connection string as the root cause and offers multiple remediation paths (DNS, PgBouncer, RDS Proxy, sidecar)
Retry gap clearly scoped to connection acquisition failures only, explicitly excluding query execution retries to avoid duplicate writes — shows operational maturity
Read replica routing correctly framed as scaling/performance optimization rather than a hard blocker
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and reasoning
Good separation of confidence areas and explicit assumptions about infrastructure context
Circuit breaker recommendation, while valid, adds scope beyond what was asked — minor over-prescription

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: manual restart identified as critical gap ('broken' status, escalated), dynamic primary resolution recommended (DNS/PgBouncer/RDS Proxy), no retry on connection error identified as reliability gap with specific backoff recommendations, read replica routing identified as optimization (correctly noted as lower confidence on must-have vs nice-to-have), verdict is NOT READY, and multiple specific recommendations provided across all failure modes.

observations.text: Correctly classified manual restart as 'broken' rather than 'risk' — strongest possible signal
Retry recommendation is specific: 1-2 retries, 100-500ms backoff — not vague
Appropriately distinguished read replica routing as lower confidence on must-have vs nice-to-have, avoiding the failure mode of over-classifying it as a blocker
Escalations correctly routed both critical gaps to Coach K with clear reasoning
Identified additional gaps beyond rubric requirements: query timeouts, connection pool metrics, circuit breakers
Confidence calibration is reasonable — high confidence on clear gaps, low confidence on traffic-dependent sizing
